sheet_name: แปลนฐานรากแผ่
source_image: image/บ้าน_เล็ก_2ชั้น_02/บ้าน_เล็ก_2ชั้น_02_หน้า31.png
view_title: แปลนฐานรากแผ่
grid_source: raw_json_ตัวที่ใช้งานจริง/04บ้าน_เล็ก_2ชั้น_02/บ้าน_เล็ก_2ชั้น_02_หน้า00_gridline.json
schema_note: Alternate foundation system to S-03 (pile footing). This sheet is the source that established the dummy grid E' in the shared grid master (a 2-footing row 1.90m before named grid E, C0-marked). Same footing/column mark separation convention as houses #2/#3.
warnings: Row A has no footing/column at col1 (only col2/col3 present).

MERGED_2026-07-20 per user rule: all 4 footing marks (F0.60x0.60, F1.30x1.30, F1.60x1.60, F1.00x1.00) were each split across 2 entries; each is now ONE entry with summed count and concatenated grid_refs (12 elements -> 8). No grid_ref appeared in more than one entry, so this is a pure merge with no change to the total footing count (16 footings before and after, and each entry's count now equals the length of its grid_refs array). This SUPERSEDES the previous warning here, which had justified keeping F1.00x1.00 split because its paired column mark differs (C1A at E3 vs C1 at B1) -- elements[] is positional-only, so the column pairing lives in its own C1A/C1 entries and is not a reason to split a footing entry.

CAUTION for anyone applying this rule elsewhere: merging is correct only when the entries' grid_refs do NOT overlap. Where the same grid_ref appears in two entries of the same mark (as found on house #3's หน้า29/หน้า30 at B3) that is a genuine double-count bug and the duplicate must be DELETED, not summed.
